paper_title: Deep Learning Methods for Improving Pollen Monitoring

methods.description: Describes the architecture, training setup, and process for training and evaluating a convolutional neural network (SimpleModel) from scratch on the ABCPollen dataset for birch, alder, and hazel pollen grain classification. This model was created during the initial stage of the study.

steps.description: Define the SimpleModel convolutional neural network architecture. The network consists of three convolutional layers, each with 4x4 filters. Input image dimensions are set to 200x200 pixels. This architecture is a modification of a model presented in a CNN tutorial [28].
steps.searchable_summary: Define a CNN model architecture with three convolutional layers, 4x4 filters, and 200x200 input size.

steps.description: Select the Adam optimizer for training the network [29].
steps.searchable_summary: Choose and configure the Adam optimizer for model training.

steps.description: Split the ABCPollen dataset into training and test sets. 1224 randomly chosen images are used for training, while the test set contained the remaining 50 samples of ABCPollen data. The number of test items was determined by the small number of images in the ABCPollen database.
steps.searchable_summary: Split a dataset into training (1224 images) and test (50 images) subsets.

steps.description: Perform the SimpleModel training process using the training set (1224 images).
steps.searchable_summary: Train the SimpleModel using the training dataset.

steps.description: Evaluate the performance of the trained SimpleModel using the test set (50 images).
steps.searchable_summary: Evaluate the trained SimpleModel on the test dataset.
